determination.primary_reasoning: Plan criteria not met; no regulatory carve-out applies; clinical standard does not strongly support surgery.

denial_specifies_criteria.reasoning: The denial notice explicitly identifies the clinical criteria applied as 'CC-SPINE-2024 (Plan Medical Policy: Cervical Spine Surgery)' and specifies the particular sections (§ 2.1 and § 2.2) used in reaching the denial decision. The notice then details how each criterion was evaluated against the submitted clinical documentation, satisfying the requirement to specify which clinical criteria from the plan policy were applied.

denial_specifies_clinical_reason.reasoning: The denial notice explicitly identifies the specific clinical findings that led to denial: intact strength (5/5), preserved reflexes, intact sensation without dermatomal distribution, negative Spurling's maneuver (no radiculopathy under § 2.1); only 4 weeks of PT instead of required 6 weeks, only OTC ibuprofen without additional agent classes, and no interventional procedures (insufficient conservative treatment under § 2.2). The notice describes which clinical fact patterns in the case failed to satisfy each criterion, not merely which criteria were applied.

denial_provides_imr_rights.reasoning: The denial notice contains a dedicated section titled 'RIGHT TO INDEPENDENT MEDICAL REVIEW' that explicitly informs the member of their right to request an IMR under California Health & Safety Code § 1374.30 and § 1374.31, provides contact information for the California Department of Managed Health Care (phone and website), and specifies the 6-month filing deadline. This satisfies the requirement that the denial notice include notice of the member's IMR rights.

cervical_radiculopathy_diagnosed.reasoning: The condition requires a confirmed diagnosis of cervical radiculopathy with nerve root compression and radicular symptoms (pain, numbness, weakness in dermatomal distribution). The clinical record explicitly states 'No radiculopathy documented,' the neurological examination shows no dermatomal sensory loss, normal strength throughout, and negative Spurling's test, and the MRI shows no nerve root impingement. All available evidence demonstrates the absence of cervical radiculopathy.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Examination dated 2026-03-18 by Dr. Elena Rodriguez: Mental status intact. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor: strength 5/5 throughout bilateral upper extremities including deltoid, biceps, triceps, wrist extension, finger flexion, intrinsic hand muscles. Sensation: intact to light touch, pinprick, and proprioception throughout C4-T1 distributions bilaterally. No dermatomal sensory loss documented. [extract] case content (confidence 0.80): MRI cervical spine, 2026-01-14: Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis. C6-C7: mild disc desiccation, no significant bulge. Impression: Mild multilevel cervical degenerative changes without significant neural compromise.

pt_contraindicated_or_futile.reasoning: The condition requires written documentation from the treating physician that physical therapy is contraindicated or unlikely to benefit the member. Dr. Rodriguez's documentation does not include any explicit attestation that physical therapy is contraindicated or unlikely to benefit the patient; rather, the physical therapy records show the patient improved during a 4-week course (pain decreased from 7/10 to 4/10, improved range of motion) before self-discontinuing treatment. The physician's pre-authorization cites 'refractory to conservative measures' but this describes past treatment history, not a documented clinical determination that PT is contraindicated or unlikely to benefit.

functional_plateau_documented.reasoning: The condition requires both completion of PT AND documented functional plateau. Here, the patient discontinued PT before completing the planned course, and the treating PT explicitly documented continued expected progress with no plateau reached. The functional_plateau_definition requires documented failure to achieve meaningful functional improvement, but the facts show 15% ROM improvement and documented ongoing gains.

structural_neurological_compromise.reasoning: The MRI shows no cord compression, no herniation, no nerve root impingement, and no central canal stenosis. The physical examination demonstrates normal motor strength (5/5), normal reflexes, intact sensation, negative Spurling's test, and no signs of radiculopathy or myelopathy. The condition requires objective evidence of structural neurological compromise (such as cord compression with deficit, significant herniation causing radiculopathy, or myelopathy) that PT cannot address; the facts affirmatively document the absence of these structural pathologies.

clinical_standard_supports_surgery.reasoning: Per tier_3_criteria, TIER 3 applies when there is absence of radiculopathy and myelopathy. This patient presents with axial neck pain and shoulder girdle pain without documented radiculopathy (negative Spurling's test, 5/5 motor strength, intact sensation throughout all dermatomes, no dermatomal sensory loss) and without myelopathy (no Hoffmann sign, no clonus, normal reflexes, MRI showing no cord compression or myelomalacia). This presentation meets the TIER 3 definition of 'axial neck pain without radiculopathy or myelopathy,' meaning surgery is not clinically indicated.
